step_1_context.text: When we report what someone said or thought, we often use a that-clause after a reporting verb:

step_2_explanation.simple_rule:
When we report what someone said or thought, we often use a that-clause after a reporting verb:
 • He said (that) he was enjoying his new job.
step_2_explanation.formula:
With other verbs we can include an object, but we must use the preposition to before it:
 • I mentioned to them that there was a problem.
step_2_explanation.common_mistake:
We can also report what someone says using other patterns:
 • verb + to-infinitive: He offered to help.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 33.1.1 The police ___ that they had captured the suspect.
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Announce doesn't need an object before the that-clause; told does.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 33.1.2 She ___ me that she would be late.
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Inform needs an object; said doesn't take an object directly before the that-clause.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 33.2.1 I ___ that I had made a mistake.
step_3_interactive_exercises.explanation_for_failure: Confess requires 'to' before the person being confessed to.

step_3_interactive_exercises.question: 33.2.2 He ___ that we should meet earlier.
33.3.1 They ___ us to leave the building immediately.
